Pachycereus centrispinus is a slow-growing cactus species that can grow up to 15 meters in height. It is a rare species found in the wild, mainly in Mexico.

Research suggests that it has several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Cereus peruvianus include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
